test_cases.description: NewAuthMiddleware returns a valid echo.MiddlewareFunc when given non-nil db.DB and PermissionRegistry arguments.
preconditions: A valid *db.DB handle connected to an in-memory SQLite database is available.
A valid *PermissionRegistry created via NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
test_cases.expected: NewAuthMiddleware returns a non-nil echo.MiddlewareFunc without panicking.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
mw = NewAuthMiddleware(db, registry)
assert mw != nil

test_cases.description: All routes registered on the APIGroup are intercepted by the auth middleware before the handler executes.
preconditions: Echo server is configured with APIGroup and auth middleware applied to it.
A test handler is registered on a route under APIGroup.
SQLite database is initialised with the required tables.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON body {"error":{"code":401,"message":"missing authorization header"}}; the test handler is never invoked.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
resp = httptest_send(GET, "/api/some-protected-route", headers={})
assert resp.status == 401
assert resp.body.error.message == "missing authorization header"
assert handler_was_invoked == false

test_cases.description: Health probe and OAuth paths are not intercepted by the auth middleware and respond normally without an Authorization header.
preconditions: Echo server has /healthz, /readyz, /version, /auth/providers, /auth/callback registered on an unprotected group.
Auth middleware is applied only to the APIGroup.
test_cases.expected: All unprotected paths respond with HTTP 200 (or their normal status); none return 401.

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'missing authorization header' when the Authorization header is absent.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
Echo test context is created with no Authorization header set.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'missing authorization header'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "missing authorization header"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid authorization header format' when the Authorization header uses a non-Bearer scheme.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
Echo test context is created with Authorization: Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid authorization header format'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Basic dXNlcjpwYXNz"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid authorization header format"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'missing token' when the Authorization header is exactly 'Bearer ' with no token.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
Echo test context is created with Authorization: Bearer  (Bearer followed by exactly one space, nothing else)
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'missing token'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer "})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "missing token"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware extracts the token string after 'Bearer ' and passes it to credential type detection when the header is well-formed.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite database has a valid admin_token_hash in admin_config.
Echo test context has Authorization: Bearer <valid_admin_token>
test_cases.expected: The middleware proceeds past extraction and invokes credential type detection (does not return 'missing authorization header', 'invalid authorization header format', or 'missing token').
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
msg = parse_json(resp.body).error.message
assert msg not in ["missing authorization header", "invalid authorization header format", "missing token"]

test_cases.description: parseToken checks admin pattern first, then PAT, then API key, and returns correct credential type for each.
preconditions: apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
test_cases.expected: parseToken returns 'admin_token', 'pat', 'api_key' respectively for the three token strings.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, _, err = parseToken("ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899")
assert err == nil and credType == "admin_token"

credType, _, err = parseToken("ak_pat_tokenid_secretval")
assert err == nil and credType == "pat"

credType, _, err = parseToken("ak_keyid_secretval")
assert err == nil and credType == "api_key"

test_cases.description: parseToken returns credential type 'admin_token' and the full token as the sole component for a valid admin token.
preconditions: apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, components, err = parseToken("ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899")
assert err == nil
assert credType == "admin_token"
assert len(components) == 1
assert components[0] == "ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"

test_cases.description: parseToken returns credential type 'pat' with token_id and secret as components for a valid PAT token.
preconditions: apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
test_cases.expected: parseToken returns ('pat', ['mytoken123', 'mysecret456'], nil).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, components, err = parseToken("ak_pat_mytoken123_mysecret456")
assert err == nil
assert credType == "pat"
assert len(components) == 2
assert components[0] == "mytoken123"
assert components[1] == "mysecret456"

test_cases.description: parseToken returns credential type 'api_key' with key_id and secret as components for a valid API key token.
preconditions: apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
test_cases.expected: parseToken returns ('api_key', ['mykey123', 'mysecretabc'], nil).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, components, err = parseToken("ak_mykey123_mysecretabc")
assert err == nil
assert credType == "api_key"
assert len(components) == 2
assert components[0] == "mykey123"
assert components[1] == "mysecretabc"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'unrecognized token format' when the token does not match any recognized pattern.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'unrecognized token format'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer totally_garbage_token"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "unrecognized token format"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware validates that the admin token hex suffix is exactly 64 valid hex characters before performing any database lookup.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite database is initialised (no admin_token_hash row required for this test).
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'; no database query is made for admin_config.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db_query_count_before = get_db_query_count()
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_tooshort"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"
assert get_db_query_count() == db_query_count_before

test_cases.description: Auth middleware computes SHA-256 of the full admin token string and uses ConstantTimeCompare against the stored hash.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite database has admin_config row with key='admin_token_hash' set to the SHA-256 hex of the full token string.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Auth middleware successfully authenticates and calls the next handler with admin AuthInfo.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
full_token = "ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"
stored_hash = hex(sha256(full_token))
db.set("admin_config", "admin_token_hash", stored_hash)
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + full_token})
resp = run_middleware_with_capture(req)
assert resp.status == 200
assert captured_auth_info.CredentialType == "admin_token"
assert captured_auth_info.Role == "admin"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware injects AuthInfo with CredentialType='admin_token', UserID='', Role='admin' when admin token hash matches.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite admin_config table has row key='admin_token_hash' with correct hash value.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Next handler is called; GetAuthInfo returns AuthInfo{CredentialType:'admin_token', UserID:'', Role:'admin', KeyID:'', TokenID:'', Permissions:nil}.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
full_token = "ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"
stored_hash = hex(sha256(full_token))
db.insert_admin_config("admin_token_hash", stored_hash)
resp, captured_ctx = run_middleware_with_ctx_capture(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer " + full_token})
auth = GetAuthInfo(captured_ctx)
assert auth.CredentialType == "admin_token"
assert auth.UserID == ""
assert auth.Role == "admin"
assert auth.KeyID == ""
assert auth.TokenID == ""
assert auth.Permissions == nil

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when admin_token_hash row is missing from admin_config.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite admin_config table has no row with key='admin_token_hash' (db.ErrNotFound is returned by the query).
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.ensure_no_row("admin_config", key="admin_token_hash")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when the admin token hash does not match the stored hash.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite admin_config table has admin_token_hash set to a different hash value than the provided token's hash.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_admin_config("admin_token_hash", hex(sha256("wrong_token_value")))
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when the API key key_id is not found in the api_keys table.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys table is empty or does not contain the key_id 'unknownkey'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.ensure_no_api_key(key_id="unknownkey")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_unknownkey_somesecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'credential revoked' when the matched API key has a non-NULL revoked_at.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys table has a row with key_id='revokedkey', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=<timestamp>.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'credential revoked'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="revokedkey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), revoked_at="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", expires_at=null, user_id="uid1")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_revokedkey_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "credential revoked"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'credential expired' when the API key expires_at is non-NULL and in the past.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys table has a row with key_id='expiredkey', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=<past timestamp>.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'credential expired'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="expiredkey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), revoked_at=null, expires_at="2000-01-01T00:00:00Z", user_id="uid1")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_expiredkey_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "credential expired"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when the API key secret hash does not match.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys table has key_id='mykey', secret_hash=sha256('correctsecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="mykey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("correctsecret")), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id="uid1")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_wrongsecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked' when the API key is valid but the owning user has status='blocked'.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys table has key_id='mykey', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id='blockeduid'.
SQLite users table has user_id='blockeduid', status='blocked'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with JSON error message 'user is blocked'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="mykey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id="blockeduid")
db.insert_user(user_id="blockeduid", status="blocked", role="user")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 403
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "user is blocked"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware injects correct AuthInfo for a valid API key after all checks pass and user is not blocked.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys has key_id='goodkey', secret_hash=sha256('goodsecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id='uid-abc'.
SQLite users has user_id='uid-abc', status='active', role='user'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Next handler is called; GetAuthInfo returns AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', UserID:'uid-abc', Role:'user', KeyID:'goodkey', TokenID:'', Permissions:nil}.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="goodkey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("goodsecret")), user_id="uid-abc")
db.insert_user(user_id="uid-abc", status="active", role="user")
resp, ctx = run_middleware_with_ctx_capture(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_goodkey_goodsecret"})
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth.CredentialType == "api_key"
assert auth.UserID == "uid-abc"
assert auth.Role == "user"
assert auth.KeyID == "goodkey"
assert auth.TokenID == ""
assert auth.Permissions == nil

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when the PAT token_id is not found in the pats table.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats table is empty or does not contain token_id='unknowntok'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.ensure_no_pat(token_id="unknowntok")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_unknowntok_somesecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'credential revoked' when the matched PAT has a non-NULL revoked_at.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats table has token_id='revokedtok', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=<timestamp>, expires_at=null, user_id='uid1'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'credential revoked'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_pat(token_id="revokedtok", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), revoked_at="2025-01-01T00:00:00Z", expires_at=null, user_id="uid1", permissions=[])
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_revokedtok_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "credential revoked"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'credential expired' when the PAT expires_at is non-NULL and in the past.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats table has token_id='expiredtok', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=<past timestamp>, user_id='uid1'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'credential expired'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_pat(token_id="expiredtok", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), revoked_at=null, expires_at="2000-01-01T00:00:00Z", user_id="uid1", permissions=[])
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_expiredtok_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "credential expired"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' when the PAT secret hash does not match.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats has token_id='mytok', secret_hash=sha256('correctsecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_pat(token_id="mytok", secret_hash=hex(sha256("correctsecret")), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id="uid1", permissions=[])
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_mytok_wrongsecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked' when the PAT is valid but the owning user is blocked.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats has token_id='mytok', secret_hash=sha256('mysecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id='blockeduid'.
SQLite users has user_id='blockeduid', status='blocked', role='user'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with JSON error message 'user is blocked'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_pat(token_id="mytok", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), user_id="blockeduid", permissions=[])
db.insert_user(user_id="blockeduid", status="blocked", role="user")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_mytok_mysecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 403
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "user is blocked"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware injects correct AuthInfo for a valid PAT after all checks pass and user is not blocked.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite pats has token_id='goodtok', secret_hash=sha256('goodsecret'), revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user_id='uid-xyz', permissions=['keys:read','tokens:read'].
SQLite users has user_id='uid-xyz', status='active', role='user'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Next handler is called; GetAuthInfo returns AuthInfo{CredentialType:'pat', UserID:'uid-xyz', Role:'user', TokenID:'goodtok', Permissions:['keys:read','tokens:read']}.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_pat(token_id="goodtok", secret_hash=hex(sha256("goodsecret")), user_id="uid-xyz", permissions=["keys:read","tokens:read"])
db.insert_user(user_id="uid-xyz", status="active", role="user")
resp, ctx = run_middleware_with_ctx_capture(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_goodtok_goodsecret"})
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth.CredentialType == "pat"
assert auth.UserID == "uid-xyz"
assert auth.Role == "user"
assert auth.TokenID == "goodtok"
assert auth.Permissions == ["keys:read", "tokens:read"]

test_cases.description: RequireAdmin returns nil when called with an admin token credential.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'admin_token', Role:'admin'} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequireAdmin(c) returns nil.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType: "admin_token", Role: "admin"})
err = RequireAdmin(ctx)
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: RequireAdmin returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden' when the authenticated credential is a regular-user API key.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', Role:'user'} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequireAdmin(c) returns an error that translates to HTTP 403 with message 'forbidden'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType: "api_key", Role: "user"})
err = RequireAdmin(ctx)
assert err != nil
assert http_status(err) == 403
assert error_message(err) == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: RequireOwnerOrAdmin returns nil when the resourceOwnerID matches the authenticated user's UUID.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', Role:'user', UserID:'uid-abc'} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequireOwnerOrAdmin(c, 'uid-abc') returns nil.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType: "api_key", UserID: "uid-abc", Role: "user"})
err = RequireOwnerOrAdmin(ctx, "uid-abc")
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: RequireOwnerOrAdmin returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden' when the user is not the resource owner and not an admin.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', Role:'user', UserID:'uid-abc'} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequireOwnerOrAdmin(c, 'uid-other') returns an error translating to HTTP 403 with message 'forbidden'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType: "api_key", UserID: "uid-abc", Role: "user"})
err = RequireOwnerOrAdmin(ctx, "uid-other")
assert err != nil
assert http_status(err) == 403
assert error_message(err) == "forbidden"

test_cases.description: RequirePermission returns nil without checking PAT permissions when the credential is an admin token or any API key.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'admin_token', Role:'admin'} or AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', Role:'user'} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequirePermission(c, 'keys', 'manage') returns nil for both admin token and API key credential types.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
for auth_info in [AuthInfo{CredentialType:"admin_token", Role:"admin"}, AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", Role:"user"}]:
  ctx = new_echo_context()
  set_auth_info(ctx, auth_info)
  err = RequirePermission(ctx, "keys", "manage")
  assert err == nil

test_cases.description: RequirePermission returns nil when the PAT includes the requested resource_type:action permission.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'pat', Permissions:['keys:read','tokens:manage']} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequirePermission(c, 'keys', 'read') returns nil.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType:"pat", Permissions:["keys:read","tokens:manage"]})
err = RequirePermission(ctx, "keys", "read")
assert err == nil

test_cases.description: RequirePermission returns HTTP 403 with 'insufficient permissions' when the PAT lacks the requested permission.
preconditions: Echo test context has AuthInfo{CredentialType:'pat', Permissions:['keys:read']} injected.
test_cases.expected: RequirePermission(c, 'keys', 'manage') returns an error translating to HTTP 403 with message 'insufficient permissions'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType:"pat", Permissions:["keys:read"]})
err = RequirePermission(ctx, "keys", "manage")
assert err != nil
assert http_status(err) == 403
assert error_message(err) == "insufficient permissions"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked' after credential validation succeeds but before calling the next handler when user status is blocked.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys has a valid, non-expired, non-revoked key for user_id='blockeduid'.
SQLite users has user_id='blockeduid', status='blocked'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: Response is HTTP 403 with 'user is blocked'; next handler is not invoked; no AuthInfo injected into context.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="mykey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("mysecret")), user_id="blockeduid")
db.insert_user(user_id="blockeduid", status="blocked", role="user")
resp, handler_called = run_middleware_tracking_handler(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_mysecret"})
assert resp.status == 403
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "user is blocked"
assert handler_called == false

test_cases.description: Blocked user check applies to both API key and PAT credential types; admin tokens are exempt.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite has a blocked user 'blockeduid' with a valid API key and a valid PAT.
SQLite admin_config has admin_token_hash for a valid admin token.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: API key and PAT for blocked user receive HTTP 403 'user is blocked'. Admin token proceeds successfully.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_user(user_id="blockeduid", status="blocked", role="user")
db.insert_api_key(key_id="k1", secret_hash=hex(sha256("s1")), user_id="blockeduid")
db.insert_pat(token_id="t1", secret_hash=hex(sha256("s2")), user_id="blockeduid", permissions=[])
resp_key = run_middleware(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_k1_s1"})
assert resp_key.status == 403
resp_pat = run_middleware(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_t1_s2"})
assert resp_pat.status == 403
admin_tok = make_valid_admin_token()
resp_admin = run_middleware(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer " + admin_tok})
assert resp_admin.status == 200

test_cases.description: NewPermissionRegistry returns a registry pre-populated with all 6 built-in permissions.
test_cases.expected: NewPermissionRegistry().List() returns exactly ['keys:manage','keys:read','orgs:read','tokens:manage','tokens:read','users:read'] in sorted order.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
list = registry.List()
assert len(list) == 6
assert list == sort(["users:read", "orgs:read", "keys:read", "keys:manage", "tokens:read", "tokens:manage"])

test_cases.description: PermissionRegistry.Register adds a valid new resource_type:action and returns nil.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
Permission 'widgets:export' is not already registered.
test_cases.expected: Register returns nil; IsValid('widgets', 'export') returns true; List includes 'widgets:export'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
err = registry.Register("widgets", "export")
assert err == nil
assert registry.IsValid("widgets", "export") == true
assert "widgets:export" in registry.List()

test_cases.description: PermissionRegistry.Register returns a non-nil error when the same resource_type:action pair is registered a second time.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
Permission 'widgets:export' has already been registered once.
test_cases.expected: Second call to Register('widgets','export') returns a non-nil error; registry state is unchanged.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
registry.Register("widgets", "export")
err = registry.Register("widgets", "export")
assert err != nil

test_cases.description: PermissionRegistry.IsValid returns true for registered permissions and false for unregistered ones.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
test_cases.expected: IsValid('users','read') returns true; IsValid('nonexistent','action') returns false.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
assert registry.IsValid("users", "read") == true
assert registry.IsValid("nonexistent", "action") == false

test_cases.description: PermissionRegistry.List returns all registered permissions as a sorted slice in ascending lexicographic order.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() with additional custom permissions 'zz:zzz' and 'aa:aaa' registered.
test_cases.expected: List() output is sorted ascending: includes 'aa:aaa' before built-ins and 'zz:zzz' at the end.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
registry.Register("zz", "zzz")
registry.Register("aa", "aaa")
list = registry.List()
assert list == sorted(list)
assert list[0] == "aa:aaa"
assert list[len(list)-1] == "zz:zzz"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware stores a fully-populated AuthInfo struct in the Echo request context after successful authentication.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
A valid API key is present in the SQLite api_keys and users tables.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
test_cases.expected: The Echo context passed to the next handler contains a non-nil AuthInfo retrievable via GetAuthInfo(c).
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="goodkey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("goodsecret")), user_id="uid1")
db.insert_user(user_id="uid1", status="active", role="user")
resp, ctx = run_middleware_with_ctx_capture(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_goodkey_goodsecret"})
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth != nil
assert auth.CredentialType != ""

test_cases.description: GetAuthInfo returns the AuthInfo pointer from a context that has been populated by the auth middleware.
preconditions: An Echo context is available with AuthInfo already injected via the middleware or manually via test setup.

input.context: Echo context with AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', UserID:'uid1', Role:'user'} injected

test_cases.expected: GetAuthInfo(c) returns a non-nil *AuthInfo with CredentialType='api_key'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
set_auth_info(ctx, AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", UserID:"uid1", Role:"user"})
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth != nil
assert auth.CredentialType == "api_key"

test_cases.description: GetAuthInfo returns nil without panicking when no AuthInfo is in the context.
preconditions: An Echo context is available with no AuthInfo injected (simulating a test without the middleware).

input.context: Echo context with no AuthInfo set

test_cases.expected: GetAuthInfo(c) returns nil without panicking.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth == nil

test_cases.description: GetUserID returns the authenticated user's UUID or an empty string for admin token or nil AuthInfo.
preconditions: Three Echo contexts: one with API key AuthInfo (UserID='uid-123'), one with admin token AuthInfo (UserID=''), one with no AuthInfo.
test_cases.expected: GetUserID returns 'uid-123', '', '' respectively.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx1 = new_echo_context_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", UserID:"uid-123"})
assert GetUserID(ctx1) == "uid-123"

ctx2 = new_echo_context_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"admin_token", UserID:""})
assert GetUserID(ctx2) == ""

ctx3 = new_echo_context()
assert GetUserID(ctx3) == ""

test_cases.description: IsAdmin returns true for admin token or admin-role API key; false for user-role API key, PAT, or nil AuthInfo.
preconditions: Multiple Echo contexts with different AuthInfo configurations.
test_cases.expected: IsAdmin returns true, true, false, false, false respectively.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
assert IsAdmin(ctx_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"admin_token", Role:"admin"})) == true
assert IsAdmin(ctx_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", Role:"admin"})) == true
assert IsAdmin(ctx_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", Role:"user"})) == false
assert IsAdmin(ctx_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"pat", Role:"user"})) == false
assert IsAdmin(new_echo_context()) == false

test_cases.description: All hash comparisons in the auth middleware use crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare and never use == or bytes.Equal.
preconditions: Source files internal/auth/auth.go, internal/auth/credentials.go are available for inspection.
Go toolchain is available.
test_cases.expected: Code inspection (grep or AST analysis) finds zero occurrences of '== ' or 'bytes.Equal' applied to hash values; at least one occurrence of 'subtle.ConstantTimeCompare' per credential validation function.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
source_files = read_files(["internal/auth/auth.go", "internal/auth/credentials.go"])
assert count_occurrences(source_files, "subtle.ConstantTimeCompare") >= 3
assert no hash comparison line uses '==' or 'bytes.Equal'

test_cases.description: hashToken computes SHA-256 of the input and returns a 64-character lowercase hex string.
preconditions: hashToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
test_cases.expected: hashToken('hello') returns '2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824'.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
result = hashToken("hello")
assert len(result) == 64
assert result == "2cf24dba5fb0a30e26e83b2ac5b9e29e1b161e5c1fa7425e73043362938b9824"
assert result == strings.ToLower(result)

test_cases.description: All auth middleware error responses use the APIError helper and produce the standard JSON error envelope.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
Request triggers an authentication error (e.g., missing Authorization header).
test_cases.expected: Response body is valid JSON with structure {"error":{"code":401,"message":"missing authorization header"}}; Content-Type is application/json.
test_cases.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={})
resp = run_middleware(req)
body = parse_json(resp.body)
assert body.error != null
assert body.error.code == 401
assert body.error.message == "missing authorization header"
assert resp.headers["Content-Type"] contains "application/json"

test_cases.description: Auth middleware only uses the approved set of error messages and no other messages are exposed to clients.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
All error-triggering scenarios can be exercised via test requests.
test_cases.expected: Every error response message is one of: 'missing authorization header', 'invalid authorization header format', 'missing token', 'unrecognized token format', 'invalid credentials', 'credential revoked', 'credential expired', 'user is blocked', 'forbidden', 'insufficient permissions', 'internal server error'.

property_tests.description: For any token starting with '<TokenPrefix>_pat_', parseToken always returns 'pat' and never 'api_key', regardless of content after the infix.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate arbitrary strings of the form 'ak_pat_' + random_alphanumeric(1..50) + '_' + random_alphanumeric(1..50) using Go's testing/quick or a fuzzer seeded with random lengths and characters.
property_tests.invariant_check:
credType, _, err = parseToken(token)
assert credType == "pat" OR err != nil
assert credType != "api_key"

property_tests.description: For any credential validation request, every hash comparison uses crypto/subtle.ConstantTimeCompare; no hash comparison uses == or bytes.Equal.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Instrument the auth middleware with a comparison counter; exercise with randomly generated admin tokens, API keys, and PAT tokens (valid and invalid).
property_tests.invariant_check:
eq_comparison_count = 0  // track plain == comparisons on hash values
subtleCompare_count = track_calls_to("subtle.ConstantTimeCompare")
assert eq_comparison_count == 0
assert subtleCompare_count >= 1 per credential validation call

property_tests.description: For any request with a non-revoked, non-expired API key or PAT whose owning user has status='blocked', the middleware always returns HTTP 403 'user is blocked' and never calls the next handler.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random key_id, secret, user_id values; insert into SQLite with revoked_at=null, expires_at=null, user status='blocked'; vary between API key and PAT credential types.
property_tests.invariant_check:
resp, handler_invoked = run_middleware_tracking_handler(request_with_blocked_user_credential)
assert resp.status == 403
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "user is blocked"
assert handler_invoked == false

property_tests.description: For any request authenticated with an admin token, AuthInfo.UserID is always empty and AuthInfo.Role is always 'admin'; GetUserID returns empty string.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random 64-character hex strings to form valid admin tokens; store their SHA-256 hash in admin_config; run middleware.
property_tests.invariant_check:
resp, ctx = run_middleware_with_ctx_capture(request_with_admin_token)
auth = GetAuthInfo(ctx)
assert auth.UserID == ""
assert auth.Role == "admin"
assert GetUserID(ctx) == ""

property_tests.description: After any call to PermissionRegistry.Register with a valid, non-duplicate permission, IsValid returns true for that permission and List includes it; no previously registered permission is removed.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random valid resource_type (lowercase letters/digits/underscores) and action strings; call Register sequentially; verify state after each call.
property_tests.invariant_check:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
before_list = registry.List()
err = registry.Register(new_resource_type, new_action)
if err == nil:
  assert registry.IsValid(new_resource_type, new_action) == true
  after_list = registry.List()
  assert new_resource_type + ":" + new_action in after_list
  for perm in before_list:
    assert perm in after_list  // no permissions removed
  assert after_list == sorted(after_list)  // still sorted

property_tests.description: RequirePermission always returns nil without inspecting the permissions list when AuthInfo.CredentialType is 'admin_token' or 'api_key', regardless of what permissions are present.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate random resource_type and action strings; set AuthInfo.CredentialType to 'admin_token' or 'api_key' with various empty/nil/non-nil Permissions lists; call RequirePermission.

property_tests.description: For any request to APIGroup that fails token extraction, parsing, or validation, the next handler is never called and AuthInfo is never injected into the context.
property_tests.for_any_strategy: Generate requests spanning all error scenarios: missing header, wrong scheme, empty bearer, unrecognized format, wrong prefix, invalid credentials, expired, revoked, blocked user.
property_tests.invariant_check:
resp, handler_invoked, ctx = run_middleware_full_capture(failing_request)
assert resp.status in [401, 403, 500]
assert handler_invoked == false
assert GetAuthInfo(ctx) == nil

edge_case_tests.description: NewAuthMiddleware panics immediately with a descriptive message when either argument is nil.
preconditions: A valid *PermissionRegistry is available.
A valid *db.DB is available.
edge_case_tests.expected: NewAuthMiddleware panics with a non-empty, descriptive message for both nil-database and nil-registry scenarios.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
assert_panics(func() { NewAuthMiddleware(nil, registry) })
assert_panics(func() { NewAuthMiddleware(db, nil) })

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware treats 'Bearer' with no trailing space or token as an invalid format and returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid authorization header format'.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid authorization header format'.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid authorization header format"

edge_case_tests.description: parseToken returns a non-nil error for an empty string input.
preconditions: parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: parseToken returns a non-nil error; credential type is empty string.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, components, err = parseToken("")
assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: parseToken returns a non-nil error when token begins with a wrong prefix instead of TokenPrefix.
preconditions: parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: parseToken returns a non-nil error.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:

assert err != nil

edge_case_tests.description: parseToken correctly identifies a token starting with '<prefix>_pat_' as 'pat' due to detection-order precedence, not 'api_key'.
preconditions: parseToken function is accessible for direct unit testing.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: parseToken returns ('pat', ['tokenid', 'secretval'], nil) — not 'api_key'.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
credType, components, err = parseToken("ak_pat_tokenid_secretval")
assert err == nil
assert credType == "pat"
assert credType != "api_key"

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware logs the error and returns HTTP 500 with 'internal server error' when the admin_config database query fails with a non-ErrNotFound error.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with a db stub that returns a generic error for admin_config queries.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
The admin token format is valid (64-char hex suffix).

input.db_behavior: admin_config query returns generic error (not db.ErrNotFound)

edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 500 with JSON error message 'internal server error'; error is logged.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db_stub = make_db_stub(admin_config_error=errors.New("disk failure"))
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_aabbccdd1122334455667788990011223344556677889900112233445566778899"})
resp = run_middleware_with_stub(req, db_stub)
assert resp.status == 500
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "internal server error"

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials' without a database lookup when the admin token hex suffix is not exactly 64 characters.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with 'invalid credentials'; no database query is made.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db_query_count_before = get_db_query_count()
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_admin_abc123"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"
assert get_db_query_count() == db_query_count_before

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 500 with 'internal server error' when the api_keys database query fails with a non-ErrNotFound error.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with a db stub that returns a generic error for api_keys queries.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 500 with JSON error message 'internal server error'.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db_stub = make_db_stub(api_keys_error=errors.New("connection lost"))
resp = run_middleware_with_stub(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_mysecret"}, db_stub)
assert resp.status == 500

apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.

input.db_behavior: api_keys lookup succeeds; users query returns generic error

edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 500 with JSON error message 'internal server error'.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db_stub = make_db_stub(api_keys_ok=true, users_error=errors.New("timeout"))
resp = run_middleware_with_stub(GET, "/", {"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_mysecret"}, db_stub)
assert resp.status == 500
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "internal server error"

Token has the API key prefix but only one component after the prefix (no underscore separator).

edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_onlyonepart"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware returns HTTP 401 with 'unrecognized token format' when a PAT token cannot be split into exactly two components after removing the pat_ infix.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
Token starts with the PAT prefix but has no underscore separator after pat_ infix.

edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_pat_onlytokenid"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401

edge_case_tests.description: RequireAdmin treats the request as unauthenticated and returns HTTP 403 with 'forbidden' when no AuthInfo is present in the context.
preconditions: An Echo context with no AuthInfo injected (GetAuthInfo returns nil).
edge_case_tests.expected: RequireAdmin(c) returns an error translating to HTTP 403 with message 'forbidden'.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
ctx = new_echo_context()
err = RequireAdmin(ctx)
assert err != nil
assert http_status(err) == 403
assert error_message(err) == "forbidden"

edge_case_tests.description: RequireOwnerOrAdmin treats empty resourceOwnerID as non-matching and returns HTTP 403 for non-admin, nil for admin.
preconditions: Two Echo contexts: one with user-role API key AuthInfo, one with admin-token AuthInfo.
edge_case_tests.expected: RequireOwnerOrAdmin(user_ctx, '') returns HTTP 403 'forbidden'; RequireOwnerOrAdmin(admin_ctx, '') returns nil.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
user_ctx = new_echo_context_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"api_key", UserID:"uid-abc", Role:"user"})
err = RequireOwnerOrAdmin(user_ctx, "")
assert err != nil
assert http_status(err) == 403

admin_ctx = new_echo_context_with_auth(AuthInfo{CredentialType:"admin_token", Role:"admin", UserID:""})
err = RequireOwnerOrAdmin(admin_ctx, "")
assert err == nil

edge_case_tests.description: A blocked user with an invalid API key secret still receives 'invalid credentials' (not 'user is blocked'), preventing credential probing.
preconditions: Auth middleware is initialised with valid db and registry.
SQLite api_keys has key_id='mykey', secret_hash=sha256('correctsecret'), user_id='blockeduid'.
SQLite users has user_id='blockeduid', status='blocked'.
apikit.TokenPrefix is set to 'ak'.
edge_case_tests.expected: Response is HTTP 401 with JSON error message 'invalid credentials' (not HTTP 403 'user is blocked').
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
db.insert_api_key(key_id="mykey", secret_hash=hex(sha256("correctsecret")), user_id="blockeduid")
db.insert_user(user_id="blockeduid", status="blocked", role="user")
req = new_request(GET, "/", headers={"Authorization": "Bearer ak_mykey_wrongsecret"})
resp = run_middleware(req)
assert resp.status == 401
assert parse_json(resp.body).error.message == "invalid credentials"

edge_case_tests.description: PermissionRegistry.Register returns a non-nil error for empty or invalid resource_type or action without modifying the registry.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
edge_case_tests.expected: Register returns a non-nil error for each invalid case; registry size remains unchanged after each failed call.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
original_count = len(registry.List())
for (rt, a) in [("","read"),("users",""),("Users","read"),("users","READ"),("my-resource","do-it")]:
  err = registry.Register(rt, a)
  assert err != nil
  assert len(registry.List()) == original_count

edge_case_tests.description: PermissionRegistry is safe for concurrent use; Register, IsValid, and List can be called from multiple goroutines without data races.
preconditions: A fresh PermissionRegistry from NewPermissionRegistry() is available.
Go test is run with the -race flag enabled.

input.concurrency: 10 goroutines each calling Register, IsValid, and List simultaneously

edge_case_tests.expected: No data race is reported by the Go race detector; all operations complete successfully.
edge_case_tests.assertion_pseudocode:
registry = NewPermissionRegistry()
wg = new_wait_group()
for i in range(10):
  go func(i):
    registry.Register("res" + str(i), "action")
    registry.IsValid("users", "read")
    registry.List()
    wg.done()
wg.wait()
// run with: go test -race ./internal/auth/...

edge_case_tests.description: The auth middleware uses the unexported contextKey type for the context key, preventing external packages from accidentally accessing AuthInfo with a plain string key.
preconditions: Source file internal/auth/context.go is available for inspection.
The authInfoKey constant is defined as type contextKey (unexported).
edge_case_tests.expected: Code inspection confirms authInfoKey is of unexported type contextKey; attempting to retrieve AuthInfo from context using a plain string key 'auth_info' returns nil.

edge_case_tests.description: Auth middleware converts both computed and stored hashes to hex-encoded byte slices before ConstantTimeCompare to ensure valid length-safe comparison.
preconditions: Source files internal/auth/credentials.go are available for inspection.
hashToken returns a lowercase hex string; stored hashes in DB are also lowercase hex strings.
edge_case_tests.expected: Both values are converted to []byte before being passed to subtle.ConstantTimeCompare; no direct string comparison of hash values occurs.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end API key authentication: client sends valid API key, middleware validates it, injects AuthInfo, and the handler reads the context successfully.
smoke_tests.trigger: HTTP GET request to a protected APIGroup endpoint with Authorization: Bearer ak_<key_id>_<secret> using a valid, non-expired, non-revoked API key for an active user.
real_components: internal/db (SQLite api_keys and users table queries)
Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (replaced with a test handler that captures AuthInfo from context)
expected_effects: Test handler is invoked exactly once.
GetAuthInfo(c) in the handler returns AuthInfo{CredentialType:'api_key', UserID:<uuid>, Role:<role>, KeyID:<key_id>}.
SQLite api_keys and users tables are queried exactly once each.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end PAT authentication with permission check: client sends valid PAT, middleware validates and injects permissions, handler calls RequirePermission and proceeds.
smoke_tests.trigger: HTTP GET request to a protected resource endpoint with Authorization: Bearer ak_pat_<token_id>_<secret> for a PAT that includes the required permission in its permissions list.
real_components: internal/db (SQLite pats and users table queries)
Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (replaced with test handler that calls RequirePermission and captures result)
expected_effects: Test handler is invoked exactly once.
GetAuthInfo(c).Permissions contains the expected permission string.
RequirePermission(c, resourceType, action) returns nil inside the handler.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end admin token break-glass authentication: infrastructure operator sends valid admin token, middleware validates hash against admin_config and grants full admin access.
smoke_tests.trigger: HTTP GET request to an admin-only endpoint with Authorization: Bearer ak_admin_<64 hex chars> where the SHA-256 hash of the full token matches admin_config.admin_token_hash.
real_components: Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (replaced with test handler that calls RequireAdmin and captures result)
expected_effects: Test handler is invoked exactly once.
RequireAdmin(c) returns nil inside the handler.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end missing Authorization header rejection: client sends request without Authorization header, middleware immediately returns HTTP 401 without invoking the handler.
smoke_tests.trigger: HTTP GET request to a protected APIGroup endpoint with no Authorization header.
real_components: Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (to verify it is never invoked)
Database (no DB queries should be made)

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end blocked user rejection: client with a valid API key (correct secret, not revoked, not expired) belonging to a blocked user receives HTTP 403.
smoke_tests.trigger: HTTP GET request with valid API key where the owning user's status in the SQLite users table is 'blocked'.
real_components: internal/auth/credentials.go (API key validation, user status check)
internal/db (SQLite api_keys and users table queries)
Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (to verify it is never invoked)
expected_effects: api_keys table is queried once; users table is queried once.
No AuthInfo is injected into the context.

smoke_tests.description: End-to-end custom permission registration and PAT usage: consuming project registers a custom permission at startup, PAT with that permission passes RequirePermission.
smoke_tests.trigger: Startup: NewPermissionRegistry() followed by registry.Register('widgets','export') and NewAuthMiddleware(db, registry). Then HTTP request with a PAT whose permissions include 'widgets:export'.
real_components: internal/db (SQLite pats and users table queries)
Echo v4 router and middleware chain
mockable: Handler function (replaced with test handler that calls RequirePermission('widgets','export'))
expected_effects: HTTP request with PAT containing 'widgets:export' receives HTTP 200.
RequirePermission(c, 'widgets', 'export') returns nil inside the handler.
All 6 built-in permissions remain registered alongside 'widgets:export'.
